title: What to know about Chamomilla recutita before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Chamomilla recutita are its application in anxiety relief, due to the presence of apigenin and luteolin, which are flavonoids that have anxiolytic properties. Chamomilla recutita is also used to treat digestive issues, as it contains apigenin and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects that can soothe the digestive tract. Additionally, the plant is known to alleviate insomnia, thanks to its chamazulene content, which has a sedative effect that promotes relaxation. Chamomilla recutita is also a traditional remedy for menstrual cramps, colic, and teething pain, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and inflammation. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat skin irritations, restlessness, and sleep disorders, while its apigenin content also helps to alleviate nausea.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Chamomilla recutita are demonstrated by its antimicrobial effects against various bacteria and fungi, which are attributed to the presence of terpenoids and flavonoids. Its antioxidant properties are also well-documented, with chamazulene and apigenin exhibiting significant free radical scavenging activity, thus protecting against oxidative stress. Additionally, the plant exhibits anti-inflammatory properties due to the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines by flavonoids, while its analgesic properties are attributed to the interaction of apigenin with opioid receptors. The sedative and antispasmodic effects of Chamomilla recutita are largely due to the GABA receptor binding of flavonoids and terpenoids, which helps to modulate the nervous system and muscle tone. Furthermore, the antihistamine and anti-allergic effects are thought to result from the inhibition of histamine release and the antioxidant activity of chamazulene, as well as its antipyretic properties, which are attributed to the reduction of prostaglandin synthesis by flavonoids.
identification: Chamomilla recutita is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its bipinnately lobed leaves and white, daisy-like flowers with yellow centers. The plant typically grows to a height of 30-90 cm and has a fibrous root system. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, but has been naturalized in other parts of the world, including North America. Chamomilla recutita prefers well-drained soil and full sun to partial shade, and is commonly found in dry, grassy areas and along roadsides. Its geographical distribution has been expanded through human activity, with the plant now being found in many parts of the world, including Australia and New Zealand.
cultivation: Chamomilla recutita is an annual herb that thrives in temperate climates with well-drained soil and full sun, growing up to 12-24 inches tall with a spread of 6-12 inches, requiring a pH range of 6.0-7.0 and moderate watering, with growth tips including providing a 60-90 day growing season, maintaining soil fertility through composting, and pruning to encourage bushy growth, while also being susceptible to root rot and other diseases if overwatered.
history: Chamomilla recutita is a plant-based medicine with a long history of use, dating back to ancient Egypt, Greece, and Rome. The essential oil extracted from its flowers was used to treat a variety of ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional European medicine, Chamomilla recutita was used to soothe digestive issues, such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as to calm skin irritations and wounds. German Commission E, a reputable herbal medicine evaluation agency, has approved the use of Chamomilla recutita for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, confirming its efficacy in treating menstrual cramps and other conditions. Clinical studies have also verified the plant's anxiolytic and antidepressant properties, making it a popular natural remedy for mood disorders.

constituents.name: Apigenin
constituents.description: A flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: A flavonoid constituent of Chamomilla recutita with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Bisabolol
constituents.description: A sesquiterpene alcohol, Bisabolol is an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant with soothing and skin-protecting properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: A flavonoid found in Chamomilla recutita, characterized by its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potential anti-cancer properties.

constituents.name: Apigenin-7-glucoside
constituents.description: Apigenin-7-glucoside is a flavone glycoside constituent of Chamomilla recutita with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin-7-glucoside
constituents.description: Luteolin-7-glucoside is a flavonoid glycoside found in Chamomilla recutita, characterized by its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: α-Bisabolol
constituents.description: α-Bisabolol is a sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are triterpene glycosides present in Chamomilla recutita, characterized by their foaming and surfactant properties.

constituents.name: Alpha-bisabolol
constituents.description: Alpha-bisabolol is a sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phospholipids
constituents.description: Phospholipids in Chamomilla recutita are phosphatidylcholine and other glycerophospholipids, which are amphipathic, emulsifying, and membrane-stabilizing molecules.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Farnesene
constituents.description: Farnesene is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Chamazulene
constituents.description: A blue, non-volatile, unsaturated sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are known to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids found in Chamomilla recutita are flavonoids and phenolic carboxylic acids with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: A constituent of Chamomilla recutita, Terpenoids exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Caryophyllene
constituents.description: A sesquiterpene constituent of Chamomilla recutita with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Volatile oils
constituents.description: A constituent of Chamomilla recutita, it contains alpha-bisabolol, alpha-pinene, and beta-pinene.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a group of compounds found in Chamomilla recutita with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Matricarin
constituents.description: Matricarin is a sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and spasmolytic properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Chamomilla recutita essential oil can exacerbate allergic reactions and skin irritation, particularly in sensitive individuals
Chamomilla recutita interacts with anticoagulants, increasing the risk of bleeding.
Chamomilla recutita counteracts spasmodic effects of other medicinal agents.
Chamomilla recutita has no established effect known as 'amplify'.
Triggers vivid dreams and lucid dreaming
Increases heart rate and blood pressure.
Increases uterine tone and causes contractions
Chronic consumption of Chamomilla recutita prolongs bleeding time
Allergic reactions in individuals with ragweed allergy due to cross-reactivity with Ambrosia artemisiifolia.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for anxiety due to its apigenin and luteolin content, which have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect and reducing anxiety symptoms.
It is used for insomnia as its apigenin and luteolin content have sedative properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
The plant is used to alleviate nausea due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with nausea.
It is used to treat menstrual cramps due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce pain associated with menstrual cramps.

The plant is used to treat digestive issues due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other digestive conditions.
It is used to alleviate stress due to its apigenin and luteolin content, which have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect and reducing stress symptoms.

The plant is used to treat skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with skin irritations.
It is used to treat inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflammation.
The plant is used to alleviate headaches due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relax the muscles and reduce pain associated with tension headaches.

It is used to treat restlessness due to its apigenin and luteolin content, which have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect and reducing restlessness symptoms.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Chamomilla recutita is used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety and insomnia, which are often linked to stress and restlessness. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties make it effective against digestive issues such as nausea and gastroenteritis, as well as skin irritations like eczema and hives. Its analgesic and antispasmodic properties also help alleviate menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and headaches, which can also be triggered by migraines and gastroesophageal reflux. Furthermore, Chamomilla recutita is used to treat allergies, colic, diarrhea, and inflammation, making it a valuable treatment option for a range of conditions. Its wound-healing properties also make it a useful treatment for wounds and gastroesophageal reflux.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Chamomilla recutita is used to treat anxiety and insomnia, often manifesting as restlessness and sleep disorders, by calming the nervous system. The plant is also applied to alleviate nausea and menstrual cramps, which can be symptoms of underlying digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, often caused by stress and inflammation. Additionally, Chamomilla recutita is used to soothe skin irritation, eczema, and hives, as well as alleviate fever and gastroenteritis, which can be triggered by allergies and gastroesophageal reflux. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat colic, headaches, diarrhea, and teething pain in infants, while also being applied topically to address inflammation, digestive issues, and gastroesophageal reflux. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of Chamomilla recutita also help to alleviate digestive issues, gastroenteritis, and eczema, making it a valuable treatment for various health conditions.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Chamomilla recutita is used to treat anxiety, menstrual cramps, and digestive issues, which are often associated with stress and restlessness. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also help alleviate headaches, inflammation, and skin irritations, as well as eczema and skin irritations caused by allergies or gastroenteritis. Additionally, its calming effects can help soothe muscle spasms and rheumatism, while also treating nausea, colic, and irritability. Furthermore, Chamomilla recutita has been used to relieve insomnia, migraines, and sleeplessness, making it a popular remedy for promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Its antispasmodic properties also help to ease digestive issues, such as gastroenteritis, and alleviate skin irritations, while also addressing migraines and colic.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Chamomilla recutita is used to treat insomnia and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. It is also applied topically to alleviate skin irritations, menstrual cramps, and eczema due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, Chamomilla recutita is used to ease nausea, digestive issues, and gastritis by soothing the stomach and promoting digestion. Additionally, it is employed to reduce fever, relieve rheumatism, and treat gastroenteritis, muscle spasms, colic, diarrhea, and headaches by modulating the body's temperature and calming the nervous system. Its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties also help to treat inflammation, gastritis, vomiting, and restlessness, making it a versatile remedy in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Chamomilla recutita is used to treat anxiety, nausea, and menstrual cramps by providing relief from stress and promoting relaxation. It is often prescribed for insomnia and restlessness to help individuals cope with digestive issues and skin irritation. Additionally, it is used to alleviate inflammation, colic, and headaches, as well as alleviate symptoms associated with allergies, such as hives and irritability. Chamomilla recutita is also used to treat muscle spasms, digestive issues, and rheumatism, while its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe gastritis and migraines, and its calming effects are also beneficial for relieving irritability and alleviating digestive problems. Further, it is used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms associated with rheumatism and gastritis.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce inflammation in the body.
The plant's essential oil contains a compound called apigenin, which has been shown to have anxiolytic effects and can help to reduce anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
The plant's soothing effects and its ability to promote relaxation can help to improve sleep quality and duration by reducing stress and anxiety levels.

The plant contains apigenin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in the digestive system, and can help to soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
The plant's essential oil has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritation, reduce redness, and promote wound healing.

The plant's essential oil contains apigenin, which has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and can help to improve cognitive function by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

The plant contains flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, and can help to boost the immune system by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

The plant's essential oil contains apigenin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help to reduce menstrual cramps by relaxing the uterine muscles and reducing prostaglandin production.
The plant's soothing effects and its ability to promote relaxation can help to reduce stress levels by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain and promoting a sense of calmness.

The plant's essential oil contains apigenin, which has been shown to have a sedative effect and can help to promote relaxation by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain and reducing anxiety levels.

benefits_description: Chamomilla recutita reduces inflammation by containing apigenin, a flavonoid that inhibits prostaglandin production, and bisabolol, a sesquiterpene that suppresses COX-2 enzyme activity, thereby alleviating inflammation. Relieving anxiety is another notable benefit, as it contains flavonoids that modulate the activity of GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels. Treats insomnia, as the plant's apigenin content promotes a restful sleep by binding to GABA receptors and enhancing sleep quality. Calming digestion is achieved through the inhibition of smooth muscle spasms and the reduction of gastric acid secretion, facilitated by the sesquiterpene bisabolol. Furthermore, soothes skin irritation, improves cognitive function, boosts immune system, prevents menstrual cramps, reduces stress levels, and promotes relaxation through the combined effects of its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and apigenin.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Chamomilla recutita improves blood flow by relaxing blood vessels due to its vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help regulate blood pressure and reduce inflammation. It reduces blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation and coagulation, a result of its anti-thrombotic and anti-coagulant properties. Chamomilla recutita increases cardiac output through its inotropic and chronotropic effects, enhancing oxygen delivery to tissues. The flavonoids present in this plant, such as apigenin and luteolin, have antioxidant properties that reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing atherosclerosis. Additionally, Chamomilla recutita increases red blood cells and improves microcirculation through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and thins blood vessels due to its vasodilatory properties.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Chamomilla recutita soothes digestion by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The plant calms inflammation through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative damage to the gut lining. Chamomilla recutita reduces nausea by inhibiting the serotonin receptors in the brain, a mechanism that also contributes to its ability to ease bloating. By relaxing gut muscles through its antispasmodic properties, the plant prevents diarrhea and treats ulcers by protecting the mucous membranes from acid damage. Chamomilla recutita heals wounds by stimulating the growth of new tissue and stimulates appetite through its antimicrobial properties, which eliminate pathogens that can suppress appetite, and also cures colitis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the colon.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Chamomilla recutita regulates hormone levels by exerting a modulating effect on the endocrine system. This action supports thyroid function by promoting the production of thyroid hormones, which are essential for metabolism and energy production. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help balance adrenal glands, reducing their workload and alleviating stress. Furthermore, Chamomilla recutita reduces cortisol production, thereby stabilizing insulin sensitivity and enhancing ovarian function, particularly in women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Additionally, the plant's ability to promote testosterone balance, relax the endocrine system, and activate the pituitary gland, which acts as the primary glandular controller of the endocrine system, makes it an effective treatment for hormonal imbalances.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Chamomilla recutita reduces inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its ability to soothe irritations in the integumentary system. The plant heals wounds due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which prevent infection and promote tissue repair. Chamomilla recutita cools burns by possessing a cooling effect, attributed to its apigenin content, and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce tissue damage. The plant eases itching caused by allergies or skin conditions, thanks to its antihistamine properties, which block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for itching. Chamomilla recutita calms skin irritations, moisturizes skin, protects the scalp from dandruff and flaking, treats acne by reducing sebum production, and fades scars due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which promote skin healing and tissue repair.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Chamomilla recutita supports lymph flow due to its apigenin content, which has been shown to modulate lymphatic function, facilitating the transport of lymph fluid. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamazulene in Chamomilla recutita reduce lymph swelling, alleviating conditions such as lymphedema. Improved lymph function is achieved through the antioxidant properties of apigenin, which protects lymph tissues from oxidative damage. Regular consumption of Chamomilla recutita has been found to boost lymph health by enhancing the immune function of lymphocytes. Additionally, the essential oil in Chamomilla recutita increases lymph circulation, tones lymph tissues, stimulates lymph drainage, relaxes lymph muscles, and regulates the lymph system due to its sedative and anti-spasmodic properties.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Chamomilla recutita reduces pain by containing sesquiterpene lactones, which inhibit prostaglandins and reduce inflammation. Relaxes tension by displaying antioxidant properties, which protect muscles from oxidative stress caused by excessive exercise or injury. Eases inflammation due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, respectively. Improves mobility and reduces swelling by promoting the production of collagen, a key component of connective tissue, and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Stimulates healing by containing apigenin, a flavonoid that enhances collagen synthesis and protects against oxidative stress, thus decreasing stiffness and promoting relaxation, relieving cramping, and cooling down joints.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Chamomilla recutita reduces anxiety through its sedative and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the nervous system. It improves sleep by regulating the body's sleep-wake cycle due to its apigenin content, a natural flavonoid with sedative and anxiolytic effects. Chamomilla recutita relaxes muscles by inhibiting the release of histamine, a neurotransmitter responsible for muscle tension, and reduces fatigue by increasing the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in regulating sleep and appetite. This plant calms nerves through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the nervous system, and alleviates stress by reducing the production of cortisol, a hormone released in response to stress. By regulating emotions and promoting relaxation, Chamomilla recutita enhances mood and supports focus through its antioxidant properties, which protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Chamomilla recutita regulates menstruation by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the uterine lining, thereby alleviating menstrual cramps and irregularities associated with dysmenorrhea. This plant reduces fertility by suppressing the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions and can disrupt ovulation, thus preventing unwanted pregnancies. Chamomilla recutita cures endometriosis by inhibiting the growth of endometrial cells and reducing inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Stimulating ovulation is another benefit, as Chamomilla recutita contains apigenin, a flavonoid that regulates the reproductive cycle by enhancing the production of gonadotropin-releasing hormone. Additionally, Chamomilla recutita prevents miscarriage and treats ectopic pregnancy, relieves dysmenorrhea, improves fertility, stimulates menstrual flow, and eases labor pains, primarily due to its spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax the uterine muscles and promote a smooth delivery.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Chamomilla recutita relieves congestion by using its antimicrobial properties to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, reducing the production of mucus. Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the respiratory system, alleviating wheezing and making it easier to breathe. Chamomilla recutita eases coughing by soothing the throat with its antioxidant properties and reducing irritation. The plant's anti-spasmodic properties decrease mucus production and prevent asthma attacks. Chamomilla recutita heals bronchitis, cures allergies, stops sneezing, and soothes the throat with its anti-allergic and anti-histamine properties, making it an effective natural remedy for various respiratory issues.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Chamomilla recutita relieves urinary incontinence by its flavonoids and essential oils' anti-inflammatory properties. Treats bladder issues, such as overactive bladder, by its flavonoids' antimicrobial properties that inhibit bacterial growth, reducing the risk of infections. Cures infections of the urinary tract by its essential oils' antimicrobial activity, preventing the spread of pathogens. Reduces inflammation in the urinary system due to its anti-inflammatory flavonoids and terpenoids that alleviate swelling and pain. Soothes irritations in the bladder and urinary tract by its anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, preventing spasms and promoting relaxation, preventing kidney damage, tightening the bladder muscles, healing ulcers in the bladder and urinary tract, and cleansing and detoxifying the urinary system to stop leaks and promote a healthy balance of fluids.

properties_list.description: Chamomilla recutita has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, particularly against certain bacteria and fungi, which may contribute to its traditional use in wound healing and skin care.
The plant contains a variety of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds that contribute to its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

Chamomilla recutita contains apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
The plant has been traditionally used as an analgesic to relieve pain, and studies have shown that its flavonoids and other compounds may contribute to its pain-relieving effects.

Chamomilla recutita has been traditionally used as a sedative to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, and studies have shown that its flavonoids and other compounds may contribute to its calming effects.
The plant has been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective effects, which may help protect against liver damage and promote liver health.

Chamomilla recutita has been traditionally used as an antispasmodic to relieve muscle spasms and cramps, and studies have shown that its flavonoids and other compounds may contribute to its relaxing effects on smooth muscle.
The plant has been shown to exhibit anticonvulsant effects, which may help prevent or alleviate seizures and other convulsive disorders.
Chamomilla recutita has been traditionally used to treat allergic reactions and may help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as hay fever and asthma.
The plant has been traditionally used to relieve pain and may help alleviate symptoms associated with a variety of conditions, including menstrual cramps, headaches, and musculoskeletal pain.

parts_description: The most used parts of Chamomilla recutita for medicinal purposes are flowers. The flowers contain the primary bioactive compound apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, as well as bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory sesquiterpene. The flowers also contain volatile oils, primarily bornyl acetate and alpha-bisabolol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties. The primary medicinal properties of the flowers include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which are beneficial for treating anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive compounds in the flowers have also been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant contain flavonoids, including apigenin and luteolin, and sesquiterpene lactones, including alpha-bisabolol and chamazulene, which possess anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and anticonvulsant properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as soothing skin irritations and reducing inflammation in wounds.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant has antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and anxiolytic properties, and is commonly used to treat menstrual cramps, insomnia, and gastrointestinal disorders.
preparations.preparation_overview: Chamomilla recutita tincture is a herbal remedy used to treat a variety of conditions including insomnia, stress, anxiety, menstrual disorders, nausea, digestive issues, hyperactivity in children, rheumatism, gastroenteritis and eczema. It's a common preparation, widely used in traditional medicine. The effects of this tincture are often reported as calming and soothing, which is likely due to its ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The preparation is made from the flowers and leaves of the chamomile plant, which are steeped in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, to extract the active compounds. Chamomilla recutita tincture is considered a difficult preparation to make as it requires precise control over the solvent-to-plant ratio and extraction time to ensure optimal potency and quality.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried flowers and leaves of Chamomilla recutita and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar, making sure they are completely covered with a solvent such as vodka or glycerin.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to aid extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle, tightly sealed and labeled, in a cool, dark place.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory and sedative properties to treat anxiety, insomnia, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, as well as to soothe digestive issues and minor wounds.
preparations.preparation_overview: Chamomilla recutita essential oil is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, headaches, irritability, inflammation, skin irritation, muscle tension, rashes, and digestive issues such as nausea. It's widely used in aromatherapy and skincare products due to its common application. The oil has a calming effect, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Chamomilla recutita essential oil is extracted from the flowers of the chamomile plant, which requires careful harvesting and distillation to produce a high-quality oil. The process is time-consuming and labor-intensive, making the oil relatively expensive.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Chamomilla recutita flowers in the early morning, when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight and moisture.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to gently heat the dried flowers, releasing their essential oils into a collection vessel.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or decanter, allowing the oil to rise to the top.
Store the resulting Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle, sealed tightly to preserve its potency and aroma.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin irritations, soothe wounds, calm eczema, and reduce inflammation and pain.
preparations.preparation_overview: Chamomilla recutita salve is a topical preparation used to treat a range of conditions including skin irritation, wound healing, burns, muscle strain, eczema, minor cuts, nursing wounds, insect bites and post-surgery pain. It's a common herbal remedy found in many first aid kits and medicine cabinets. One of the common effects of using this salve is the reduction of inflammation and promotion of healing. The preparation is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Chamomilla recutita plant, which is also known as German chamomile. The process of creating this salve can be challenging due to the need for precise extraction and drying of the plant material to ensure the desired potency and effectiveness.
preparation_recipe: Gather a sufficient quantity of fresh Chamomilla recutita flowers and leaves, clean and dry them.
Infuse the dried flowers and leaves in a carrier oil, such as olive or jojoba oil, for several weeks, allowing the mixture to steep and extract the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Combine the infused oil with a natural wax, such as beeswax, and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is fully melted and incorporated.
Pour the melted salve mixture into small containers, allowing it to cool and solidify into a smooth, herbal salve.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used for the local relief of itching, irritation, and inflammation of the rectum and anus, and for the treatment of mild to moderate hemorrhoids.
preparations.preparation_overview: Chamomilla recutita suppository is a medicinal preparation used to treat various conditions such as hemorrhoids, anal itching, fissures, proctitis and rectal inflammation. It's an uncommon used treatment option, however, practitioners may prescribe it for its efficacy. The preparation has an uncommon effect, which is attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Chamomilla recutita suppository is made from dried flowers and leaves of the plant, which are extracted and formulated into a suppository. The manufacturing process is complex and requires precise control of temperature and humidity to preserve the active ingredients.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean Chamomilla recutita flowers and leaves according to the desired quantity.
Dry the flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a natural binding agent, such as beeswax or coconut oil, to facilitate the suppository formation.
Fill suppository molds with the mixture and allow them to cool and harden before use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used for the short-term management of insomnia, anxiety, restlessness, and mild dental pain, as well as to induce sedation before medical procedures.
preparations.preparation_overview: Chamomilla recutita capsule is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, inflammation, digestive issues, skin irritation, stress, allergies, menstrual cramps and restlessness. It's widely used due to its effectiveness in alleviating these symptoms. The common effect of using Chamomilla recutita capsule is relief from these conditions. The capsule is made from dried flowers of the chamomile plant, which contain active compounds like apigenin and luteolin that are responsible for its medicinal properties. The process of making Chamomilla recutita capsule involves drying and processing the flowers, which is a moderately difficult process requiring careful handling to preserve the active ingredients.
preparation_recipe: Collect fresh or dried Chamomilla recutita flowers from a trusted source.
Clean and dry the Chamomilla recutita flowers thoroughly to remove any debris or impurities.
Crush the dried Chamomilla recutita flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent, such as gelatin or starch, to help form the capsule.
Fill a capsule shell with the Chamomilla recutita powder mixture and seal the capsule.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to alleviate insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness, and to soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome and menstrual cramps.
preparations.preparation_overview: Chamomilla recutita tea is a herbal preparation used to treat conditions such as colic, anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, headache, digestion problems, nausea and menstrual issues, gastroesophagitis. This is a very common preparation used worldwide. Its effects are well-documented, with a common side effect being drowsiness. The preparation is made from the flowers of the Matricaria chamomilla plant. It's easy to make, as the flowers are steeped in hot water to release their active compounds, which are then consumed as a tea.
preparation_recipe: Gather a sufficient quantity of fresh or dried Chamomilla recutita flowers.
Rinse the flowers with cold water to remove any impurities.
Combine the cleaned Chamomilla recutita flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for a few minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the liquid and discard the flowers to obtain the Chamomilla recutita herbal tea.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Chamomilla recutita are exacerbate allergies and allergic reactions, interact with anticoagulant medications such as warfarin, counteract the effects of certain medications used to treat anxiety and sleep disorders, amplify the risk of uterine contractions and bleeding during pregnancy, induce symptoms of ragweed allergy in individuals with sensitivities, evoke an immune response in people with autoimmune disorders, accelerate the heart rate and blood pressure in sensitive individuals, contribute to the development of a rare but serious condition called anaphylaxis, prolong the use of medications that have a narrow therapeutic index, and trigger severe gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and vomiting.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Chamomilla recutita may cause uterine stimulation and lead to miscarriage or premature labor.
Chamomilla recutita may cause drowsiness and sedation in infants, potentially affecting suckling.
Chamomilla recutita interacts with sedatives, anticoagulants, and diabetes medications, potentially exacerbating their effects.
Contact dermatitis, oral allergy syndrome, and anaphylaxis have been reported in individuals allergic to Chamomilla recutita.
Chamomilla recutita contains apigenin, which is toxic to infants under 2 years.
Chamomilla recutita may worsen autoimmune conditions such as lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, and multiple sclerosis by stimulating the immune system.
Large doses cause stomach upset and diarrhea, especially in children.
Chamomilla recutita contains apigenin and other flavonoids, which have sedative properties that can cause drowsiness.
Chamomilla recutita may interact with herbs like St. John's Wort, affecting serotonin levels and increasing risk of serotonin syndrome.
Chamomilla recutita may interact with anticoagulant and antiplatelet medications, including warfarin, aspirin, and clopidogrel.

intro_study: According to a study published by Phytotherapy research: PTR, Chamomilla recutita exhibits significant antiinflammatory action, as demonstrated by animal model studies. Additionally, it shows potent antispasmotic and anxiolytic effects, making it a potential treatment for anxiety-related disorders. Furthermore, its antioxidant and antimicrobial activities suggest its potential in protecting against oxidative stress and infections.

According to a study published by "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", Chamomilla recutita has been found to possess several pharmacological properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antiseptic, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which contribute to its medicinal and aromatic properties. The flower heads of Chamomilla recutita are used to prepare a well-known herbal tea for mind and body relaxation, further highlighting its benefits. These properties make Chamomilla recutita a highly sought-after herb in the pharma and aroma industries, where it is valued for its medicinal and aromatic uses.

According to a study published by "International journal of molecular sciences", the extracts of Chamomilla recutita have shown anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antitumor properties. The extracts possessed variable antioxidant potential, in terms of decreased radical generation and increased reduced glutathione levels, due to the presence of flavonoids and cinnamic acid derivatives. Additionally, flavones such as apigenin and luteolin were found to be highly represented in the extracts, contributing to their biological activity.

According to a study published by Phytotherapy Research, PTR, the extracts of Chamomilla recutita were found to be safe for human use and exhibited significant antihypertensive activity, leading to a reduction in both systolic and diastolic blood pressure in normotensive and hypertensive human volunteers. Additionally, the extracts improved serum biochemical parameters and lipid profile levels, as well as restored the amount of reduced Glutathione in tissues and increased the activity of superoxide dismutase (SOD). Furthermore, the study found a significant dose-dependent decrease in systolic blood pressure, diastolic blood pressure, and heart rate in human volunteers after oral administration of Chamomilla recutita beverages.

According to a study published by "EXCLI journal", the use of Chamomilla recutita has shown certain positive effects in the prevention of obesity and complications of diabetes. These effects were associated with the modulation of signaling pathways involving various transcription factors, resulting in potential anti-obesity actions. Furthermore, the high content of phenolic compounds and essential oils in Chamomilla recutita preparations may contribute to its pharmacological effects, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions.

According to a study published by "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", Chamomilla recutita has been found to possess various beneficial properties, including the ability to treat mild skin diseases and inflammation, as well as alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation. The study also revealed that the extracts of this plant exhibit analgesic activity, reducing pain in rodents. Furthermore, the extracts were found to have soporific effects, improving sleep quality, making Chamomilla recutita a valuable resource for the pharmaceutical industry.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", the extracts of Chamomilla recutita have been found to possess antioxidant activity, with polar extracts revealing IC50 values ranging from 13 to 57 µg/mL in the DPPH radical scavenging assay. Additionally, these extracts have shown mild antibacterial effects against Staphylococcus aureus and Bacillus subtilis, demonstrating their potential in combating bacterial infections. Furthermore, the antioxidant activity of Chamomilla recutita extracts was found to be comparable to that of its flower extracts, highlighting its value as a sustainable source of bioactive phytochemicals.

According to a study published by "Journal of Cancer", the extract of Chamomilla recutita flowers has shown good antioxidant and anticancer activities. The results demonstrated that various extracts of Chamomilla recutita contained strong anticancer properties, with ethyl acetate extract exhibiting the highest activity against prostate cancer cell lines. Additionally, the antioxidant activity of Chamomilla recutita was confirmed through the DPPH method, further highlighting its potential as a natural anti-cancer compound.

According to a study published by "BMC research notes", the ethanolic extract of Chamomilla recutita exhibited antioxidant activity, with a scavenging activity of up to 94.8% at a concentration of 1.50 mg/mL. It also showed potential as a cancer preventive agent by regulating tumor angiogenesis and inhibiting the expression of certain proteins involved in the process. Additionally, the extract demonstrated a dose-dependent reduction in cell viability against human hepatoma (HepG2) cancer cells, with an IC50 of approximately 300 µg/mL.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Chamomilla recutita are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is mediated by volatile oils and flavonoids, enabling it to inhibit the growth of various pathogens. Its antioxidant properties, primarily due to the presence of apigenin and luteolin, help to neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative damage, while its anti-inflammatory effects, largely attributed to bisabolol, reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, the plant exhibits analgesic and sedative properties, with sesquiterpenes and flavonoids contributing to its ability to relieve pain and promote relaxation. Chamomilla recutita also possesses hepatoprotective properties, as a result of its ability to modulate liver enzymes and reduce oxidative stress, and exhibits antispasmodic and anticonvulsant effects due to the presence of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce muscle spasms and prevent seizure activity. Furthermore, the plant's antiallergic properties, largely attributed to its flavonoid content, enable it to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators involved in allergic reactions, providing relief from conditions such as hay fever and atopic dermatitis, and its pain relief properties, mediated by volatile oils and flavonoids, enable it to reduce pain and discomfort associated with various conditions.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Chamomilla recutita are apigenin, luteolin, bisabolol, quercetin and apigenin-7-glucoside, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and soothing properties. Apigenin exhibits anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, while luteolin has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Bisabolol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, while quercetin, a flavonoid, demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Apigenin-7-glucoside has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, similar to apigenin. These constituents contribute to the plant's ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Chamomilla recutita for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are often used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to their sedative properties, while essential oils, typically used topically for their anti-inflammatory effects, are also derived from this plant. A salve, a topical preparation, is used to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation. Suppositories, used to treat digestive issues, are also made from Chamomilla recutita, and are often used to treat irritable bowel syndrome and other gastrointestinal problems. Capsules, typically used to treat anxiety and insomnia, contain dried flower heads of the plant and are often used as a natural remedy for stress relief. Chamomilla recutita tea, used to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation, is often consumed to soothe the stomach and promote a restful night's sleep.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Chamomilla recutita medicinally are avoiding it in pregnancy due to potential effects on the fetus, using it with caution in breastfeeding as it may transfer into breast milk. Additionally, individuals should be aware that Chamomilla recutita may interact with medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, and may cause allergic reactions in rare cases. Those with children under the age of 2 should also exercise caution and consult a healthcare professional before administering the herb, as it may exacerbate autoimmune conditions and cause drowsiness due to its sedative properties. Furthermore, interactions with other herbs, such as those with a similar mechanism of action, are also possible and should be monitored. Consulting a healthcare professional before using Chamomilla recutita medicinally, especially when using it in moderation, is essential to minimize potential risks.
intro_description: Chamomilla recutita, commonly known as German chamomile, is a medicinal herb primarily used to alleviate symptoms associated with anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, and digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The main health benefits of this herb include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and acting as a natural anti-spasmodic agent. Its therapeutic properties are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, and anti-spasmodic effects, which contribute to its efficacy in calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation. The bioactive compounds responsible for these properties include apigenin, luteolin, and bisabolol, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant activities. German chamomile is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, which is commonly used to calm the mind and body, and is also used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
